source_docs.title: Full-length RNA-seq from single cells using Smart-seq2

oligos.name: Oligo-dT30VN RT primer
oligos.role: reverse-transcription priming on poly(A) RNA; 25-nt universal 5' anchor + 30-nt poly-dT + anchoring VN
oligos.notes: 5'-AAGCAGTGGTATCAACGCAGAGTAC (25-nt anchor) + T30 + VN, where V = A/C/G and N = any base. The VN anchors the primer to the start of the poly(A) tail. Used at 10 uM (1 ul per reaction).

oligos.name: Template-switching oligo (LNA, rGrG+G)
oligos.role: template switching; adds a common 5' anchor sequence to the cDNA 3' end
oligos.notes: Written 5'-AAGCAGTGGTATCAACGCAGAGTACATrGrG+G-3'; the 3' end carries two riboguanosines (rG rG) and one LNA-modified guanosine (+G), folded here to GGG. This is the Smart-seq2 TSO variant; other tested TSOs (rGrGrG, rGrG+N, +G+G+G, etc.) are listed in Supplementary Table 2. Used at 100 uM stock / 1 uM final.

oligos.name: ISPCR primer (IS PCR)
oligos.role: single-primer PCR preamplification of full-length cDNA (matches the common anchor on both cDNA ends)
oligos.notes: 5'-AAGCAGTGGTATCAACGCAGAGT-3' (first 23 nt of the shared anchor). Both the oligo-dT and TSO carry this sequence, so a single primer amplifies full-length cDNA. Used at 0.1 uM final.

oligos.name: Nextera Tn5 mosaic end (ME) duplex
oligos.role: Tn5 transposase recognition/mosaic-end; inserted into cDNA during tagmentation
oligos.notes: Standard Illumina/Nextera 19-bp mosaic end from the Nextera XT DNA sample prep kit (not printed in the protocol text; canonical Nextera sequence).

oligos.name: Nextera transposome Read 1 oligo (i5 side)
oligos.role: adapter added by Tn5; provides Read 1 sequencing-primer binding site + ME
oligos.notes: Read 1 primer region (TCGTCGGCAGCGTC) + mosaic end (AGATGTGTATAAGAGACAG). Standard Nextera XT adapter; extended to full P5/i5 by the N5xx enrichment primer.

oligos.name: Nextera transposome Read 2 oligo (i7 side)
oligos.role: adapter added by Tn5; provides Read 2 sequencing-primer binding site + ME
oligos.notes: Read 2 primer region (GTCTCGTGGGCTCGG) + mosaic end (AGATGTGTATAAGAGACAG). Standard Nextera XT adapter; extended to full P7/i7 by the N7xx enrichment primer.

oligos.name: Nextera XT Index 2 primer (N5xx, i5)
oligos.role: enrichment-PCR primer adding the P5 flow-cell adapter and the i5 sample index
oligos.notes: Nextera XT 24-index kit (FC-131-1001). Exact index barcodes not printed in the documents; represented as a placeholder.

oligos.name: Nextera XT Index 1 primer (N7xx, i7)
oligos.role: enrichment-PCR primer adding the P7 flow-cell adapter and the i7 sample index
oligos.notes: Nextera XT 24-index kit (FC-131-1001). Exact index barcodes not printed in the documents; represented as a placeholder.

final_library.annotated_library_sequence: AATGATACGGCGACCACCGAGATCTACAC (P5) [SAMPLE_INDEX:8] (i5) TCGTCGGCAGCGTC (Read1) AGATGTGTATAAGAGACAG (ME) [CDNA] CTGTCTCTTATACACATCT (ME') CCGAGCCCACGAGAC (Read2rc) [SAMPLE_INDEX:8] (i7) ATCTCGTATGCCGTCTTCTGCTTG (P7')
annotation_lines: cDNA insert (random fragment of full-length cDNA): [CDNA]

library_generation.title: Cell lysis & oligo-dT annealing
library_generation.summary: A single cell is lysed and the tailed oligo-dT30VN primer hybridizes to the poly(A) tail of each mRNA.
library_generation.note: Cells are picked into hypotonic Triton X-100 lysis buffer with RNase inhibitor, dNTPs and oligo-dT30VN, then heated to 72 C for 3 min to denature RNA secondary structure and anneal the primer.

library_generation.title: Reverse transcription (first strand)
library_generation.summary: Superscript II extends the oligo-dT primer to make full-length first-strand cDNA and adds 2-5 untemplated C's at the 3' end.
library_generation.note: RT at 42 C for 90 min with betaine and elevated MgCl2, cycling 50 C/42 C to melt secondary structure. M-MLV terminal-transferase activity adds a few untemplated cytosines when it reaches the 5' cap.

library_generation.title: Template switching
library_generation.summary: The TSO base-pairs with the untemplated C's and the RT switches template, copying the TSO to append the common anchor to the cDNA 3' end.
library_generation.note: The LNA (+G) TSO anneals strongly to the 3' C-overhang; the polymerase reads through it, so both ends of the cDNA now carry the AAGCAGTGGTATCAACGCAGAGT... anchor.

library_generation.title: PCR preamplification
library_generation.summary: A single ISPCR primer amplifies the full-length cDNA because both ends carry the shared anchor sequence.
library_generation.note: KAPA HiFi HotStart, 18 cycles for single cells (no bead purification of first strand). Yields ~1-30 ng of cDNA with a ~1.5-2 kb size peak.
library_generation.product:
5'- AAGCAGTGGTATCAACGCAGAGTAC...[full-length cDNA]...ACTCTGCGTTGATACCACTGCTT -3'
3'- TTCGTCACCATAGTTGCGTCTCA...[full-length cDNA]...TGAGACGCAACTATGGTGACGAA -5'
        <-------- ISPCR primer amplifies both ends -------->

library_generation.title: Tagmentation (Nextera XT Tn5)
library_generation.summary: Tn5 transposase simultaneously fragments the amplified cDNA and inserts Nextera Read1/Read2 mosaic-end adapters.
library_generation.note: 55 C, 5 min; Tn5 then stripped with NT buffer. Fragments average ~200-600 bp and carry a mosaic end on each side.
library_generation.product:
5'- TCGTCGGCAGCGTCAGATGTGTATAAGAGACAG [cDNA fragment] CTGTCTCTTATACACATCTCCGAGCCCACGAGAC -3'
3'- AGCAGCCGTCGCAGTCTACACATATTCTCTGTC [cDNA fragment] GACAGAGAATATGTGTAGAGGCTCGGGTGCTCTG -5'

library_generation.title: Enrichment PCR (dual index)
library_generation.summary: N5xx (i5/P5) and N7xx (i7/P7) primers add flow-cell adapters and sample indices, gap-filling the ME and completing the sequenceable library.
library_generation.note: Limited-cycle PCR (e.g. 12 cycles for ~100 pg input), AMPure XP cleanup, pooled so no two samples share the same N5xx+N7xx combination (up to 96-plex).

library_sequencing.note: Reads into the random cDNA fragment. Paper used 43-bp reads on HiSeq 2000; protocol also runs 50-bp single-end. No UMI, not strand-specific.
library_sequencing.diagram:
Read 1 primer 5'-TCGTCGGCAGCGTCAGATGTGTATAAGAGACAG--------->  (reads cDNA)
                    |||||||||||||||||||||||||||||||||
5'- ...TCGTCGGCAGCGTCAGATGTGTATAAGAGACAG[cDNA insert]CTGTCTCTTATACACATCT... -3'  (top)
                                        |||||||||||||          |||||||||||||||||||
3'- ...AGCAGCCGTCGCAGTCTACACATATTCTCTGTC[cDNA insert]GACAGAGAATATGTGTAGA... -5'  (bottom, template)

library_sequencing.note: i7 sample index read (Nextera XT, 8 bp). Reads from the mosaic-end/Read2 handle into the i7 index.

library_sequencing.primer: Nextera Index 2 primer (anneals to the Read 1 handle, TCGTCGGCAGCGTC)
library_sequencing.note: i5 sample index read (Nextera XT, 8 bp). Reads from the Read 1 handle back into the i5 index; used with i7 for dual-index demultiplexing.
Optional paired-end read into the cDNA fragment from the opposite end.

title: SMART-seq2
description: SMART-seq2 is a plate-based, full-length single-cell RNA-seq method for polyadenylated transcripts. A tailed oligo-dT primer reverse-transcribes mRNA with Superscript II; template switching via an LNA-containing TSO appends a common 5' anchor to the cDNA 3' end, so a single ISPCR primer PCR-preamplifies full-length cDNA. Amplified cDNA is fragmented and adapter-tagged in one step by the Nextera XT Tn5 transposase, then dual-index (i5/i7) enrichment PCR builds the sequenceable library. Reads cover the entire transcript body (no UMIs, not strand-specific); index reads assign each fragment to its cell of origin.

reference.label: Full-length RNA-seq from single cells using Smart-seq2

original_publication.title: Smart-seq2 for sensitive full-length transcriptome profiling in single cells

authors.name: Simone Picelli
authors.affiliation: Ludwig Institute for Cancer Research, Stockholm, Sweden

throughput.summary: Plate-based, one cell per reaction; up to 96 dual-indexed libraries pooled per Illumina lane. 262 single cells profiled in the study.
throughput.cells: Single cells (159 HEK293T, 34 DG-75, 30 C2C12, 39 MEF); RNA content <10-16 pg/cell; 1-20 million reads per cell
throughput.rna: Poly(A)+ mRNA only; ~1-30 ng amplified full-length cDNA per single cell
throughput.dna: Final tagmented library ~300-800 bp; loaded at 2 nM

publication.statistical_model: Expression quantified as RPKM (full-length, no UMIs); gene-expression values treated as log-normally distributed across single cells, with Student's t-test and Wilcoxon rank-sum tests for significance and adjusted-Wald confidence intervals for detection rates.

other.label: Companion protocol
other.value: Picelli et al., Full-length RNA-seq from single cells using Smart-seq2, Nature Protocols 9, 171-181 (2014), doi:10.1038/nprot.2014.006

other.label: Key innovation
other.value: Single LNA guanylate (+G) at the TSO 3' end (rGrG+G) roughly doubles cDNA yield vs SMARTer IIA; betaine + 9-12 mM Mg2+ adds further 2-4x

other.label: Bead-free preamplification
other.value: KAPA HiFi HotStart amplifies first-strand cDNA directly (no bead cleanup), increasing average cDNA length by ~450 nt

other.label: Limitations
other.value: Poly(A)+ RNA only; not strand-specific; no UMIs/molecule counting; late pooling after enrichment PCR

data_processing.summary: The protocol prescribes a standard full-length scRNA-seq pipeline: raw base-calls are exported to FastQ with CASAVA, reads are demultiplexed to their cell of origin using the dual index reads, then aligned to genome and transcriptome with an RNA-seq aligner and quantified per gene/transcript (the paper points to the TopHat/Cufflinks protocol as the dedicated example). No UMI collapsing is possible (no UMIs) and reads are not strand-specific.
